markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Plan product-code rename from ModelManual/Model Manual/model_manual to UserManual with explicit deprecated shims and tests. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualNamingMigrationPlan leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualNamingMigrationPlan is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 fcf9bce3. Canonical user_manual module (src/user_manual/mod.rs) + machine-readable naming migration plan (src/user_manual/migration_plan.rs): 16 typed PlanRows (UMMIG-001..016) covering EVERY *model_manual* file in the crate (modules, kernel002 struct, atelier merge, Tauri commands, IPC channels, projections, generator binary, tests, MANUAL_VERSION constant) with phase (P1 canonical authority / P2 app rename / P3 file retirement), shim_state, owner lane, pinning test, and named blocker; 7 LegacyAlias rows mapping every legacy callable to its canonical /usermanual surface. Plan is runtime-discoverable (GET /usermanual/migration-plan) and seeded as user_manual_legacy_aliases authority rows. Tests GREEN on real PG: lib user_manual::migration_plan (3: plan_row_ids_are_unique_and_fields_populated, aliases_are_unique_and_carry_deprecation_notes, legacy_tauri_and_ipc_surfaces_each_have_an_alias) + user_manual_storage_tests::mt193_every_legacy_model_manual_file_is_plan_covered (recursive src/+tests/ file scan vs plan rows) + mt193_every_legacy_alias_resolves_to_canonical (every DB alias resolves to a registered route/stored page). Constraint honored: model_manual module untouched (app #[path]-include safety); legacy remains a declared deprecated shim with deterministic mapping per spec 10.15.8. HBR-MAN-001/002/003: naming + mapping + receipts law proven for the bridge; build-rule enforcement is MT-195.

concerns_found_and_fixed: File-scan gate (RED first run) caught 3 legacy files the hand-written plan missed: src/kernel/dcc_kb003_model_manual_hints.rs, tests/atelier_model_manual_merge_tests.rs, tests/kernel_model_manual_tests.rs -> UMMIG-014/015/016 added; gate re-run GREEN. The gate provably fails on uncovered legacy surfaces.

Did NOT use #[deprecated] attributes on model_manual items: live consumers (kernel acceptance run, atelier merge, app include) would warn/deny-break; declared-deprecation lives in the typed plan + DB alias rows + bridge receipts instead, which spec 10.15.8 accepts (deterministic mapping + compatibility receipt).
residual_risks: P2 (Tauri command rename) and P3 (file retirement) are tracked plan rows owned by frontend/later-WP lanes; blockers are named per row, not silent.

adversarial_review_v2.summary: Follow-up adversarial review found no blocking findings after durable ModelManual-to-UserManual wording, USER_MANUAL_VERSION HBR-MAN-001 guidance, Loom route parity, full-row freshness, receipt atomicity, and fail-closed PostgreSQL test fixes.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-193 requires a concrete, restartable, machine-readable ModelManual->UserManual naming migration plan with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, named files/blockers, deprecated-shim mapping, and validator-readable test evidence. The code delivers exactly that: a typed 16-row PlanRow plan plus 7 LegacyAlias rows in migration_plan.rs, seeded into real PostgreSQL (user_manual_legacy_aliases, migration 0310) directly from the plan, and enforced by a recursive file-scan gate and a real-PG alias-resolution test.
findings: migration_plan.rs:164-357 defines 16 typed PlanRows (UMMIG-001..016) covering every *model_manual* surface in the crate (modules, kernel002 struct, atelier merge, Tauri commands, IPC channels, generated projection, generator binary, tests, MANUAL_VERSION constant); each row names legacy_path, canonical_ref, phase, shim_state, owner_lane, migration_test, and an explicit blocker. This satisfies the MT acceptance criteria (exact files, constraints, failure modes, named blockers).

migration_plan.rs:359-409 defines 7 LegacyAlias rows; seed.rs:1786-1788 (seed_aliases) reads them DIRECTLY from naming_migration_plan().aliases, so the typed plan is the single seed source for the PG authority table - no duplicate second source.

PG authority is real: migrations/0310_user_manual.sql:176-186 creates user_manual_legacy_aliases with CHECK constraints and a FK to kernel_event_ledger; store.rs:875-934 implements upsert/get/list against that table; seed.rs:1835-1840 writes the alias rows. Durable-authority claim is backed by real PostgreSQL, not SQLite/in-memory/mock (constraint honored).

Test proof is real and adversarial: user_manual_storage_tests.rs:54 mt193_every_legacy_model_manual_file_is_plan_covered recursively scans src/ and tests/ for *model_manual* files and FAILS if any lacks a plan row (the split-brain gate); :173 mt193_every_legacy_alias_resolves_to_canonical runs against real PG (skip_if_no_pg! via POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL, knowledge_pg_support), seeds the corpus, and asserts every seeded alias resolves to a registered /usermanual route or stored page/tool. migration_plan.rs:411-463 adds 3 unit tests for row/alias uniqueness and populated fields.

Spec alignment confirmed: anchors 10.15.8 (UserManual migration bridge) and 2.3.13.11 (UserManualRecord) resolve in current v02.194 (02-system-architecture.md, 10-product-surfaces.md). Module law in mod.rs:1-73 keeps user_manual canonical and model_manual a declared deprecated shim, matching the MT constraint.

Spec-Realism Gate: sub_rule_1 PASS - grep of migration_plan.rs/seed.rs/storage tests found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-' escapes reachable from the deliverable (PendingRename is a tracked ShimState enum with a named blocker, not a code escape). sub_rule_2 PASS - real Handshake-owned PG resource (user_manual_legacy_aliases table + EventLedger version receipt) is touched by the seed and exercised by the real-PG test. sub_rule_3 PASS - lifecycle.completed_by is null and status is READY_FOR_VALIDATION; implementer did not self-certify COMPLETED.

Aside (non-blocking, governance paperwork): the MT's self-authored adversarial_code_review/adversarial_review_v2 blocks carry no validator authority; their described fixes (file-scan gate catching 3 missed files -> UMMIG-014/015/016) are independently verifiable in the code and the enforcing test, so they are accurate but not relied upon for this verdict.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
